hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic complementarity, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding but are not sufficiently diverse to avoid off-target interactions. Targeted mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to enhance selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations at these positions can help reduce off-target binding.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining the overall scaffold and pharmacophore integrity.
